task: Write a Python function title_words(s) returning the string with every whitespace-separated word title-cased (first letter upper, the rest lower) and runs of whitespace collapsed to single spaces, except that words already entirely uppercase stay exactly as they are. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function correctly implements the title-casing and whitespace collapsing logic, and the code is clear and well-structured.
trace: I used a list comprehension to iterate over each word in the input string `s` after splitting it by whitespace. For each word, I checked if it was already entirely uppercase. If so, I kept it as is. Otherwise, I title-cased it (making the first letter uppercase and the rest lowercase). Finally, I joined these processed words back into a single string with spaces in between. The edge case of handling a single uppercase word is demonstrated with the print statement at the end.
